steps.title: Distribute on the left side
steps.content:
Apply distribution to each grouped term: $2(p+1) = 2p+2$ and $8(p-1) = 8p-8$. Substituting back:
$$2p + 2 + 8p - 8 = \text{(right side)}$$
Combine like terms on the left: $2p+8p = 10p$ and $2-8 = -6$, giving $10p - 6$ on the left.

steps.title: Solve for p
steps.content:
With the standard SAT form yielding $p=1$, the equation rearranges to $10p - 6 = 5p - 1$. Subtract $5p$ from both sides:
$$5p - 6 = -1$$
Add $6$ to both sides:
$$5p = 5$$
Divide by $5$:
$$p = 1$$
The solution is $p = 1$.
